queue_item.action: Create a privacy-safe Long Animation Frames evidence classifier for UI-thread congestion and rendering pressure.

progress.source_status: Editor's Draft dated 16 April 2026; work in progress
progress.finding: Long-animation-frame observations can expose frame-correlated UI-thread congestion and rendering pressure, but absence claims require supported observation, callback confirmation, and zero dropped entries.
progress.verification: 6 deterministic Node.js tests passed in an isolated runtime using byte-identical source and test content.
progress.privacy_boundary: Only aggregate counts and durations are retained; script URLs, function names, DOM identifiers, raw entries, page text, private chat, and health data are excluded.
progress.uncertainty: The specification is an editor's draft, browser support is target-dependent, and project severe thresholds are heuristics rather than specification thresholds.

queue_item.handoff: Vercel Studio should collect long-animation-frame observations around gesture-driven visual transitions and attach the privacy-safe summary to performance evidence. Unsupported, unconfirmed, or dropped-entry sessions cannot prove absence of jank.

coordination.reason: Append-only update preserves concurrent team state and the canonical queue's historical snapshot.
